title: QUIC RFC 9000 DATA_BLOCKED Runtime Emission Verification
verification_method: Requirement-home execution, protected-packet inspection, existing DATA_BLOCKED frame-codec fuzz coverage, BenchmarkDotNet Dry validation, and SpecTrace render/check for the bounded active-runtime `DATA_BLOCKED` emission slice.
scope: This verification covers active-runtime advisory `DATA_BLOCKED` emission when an application stream write is blocked by the peer's current connection-level flow-control limit. It also covers suppression while ack-eliciting work remains in flight and confirms loss-repair retention for the protected advisory packet.
expected_result: The requirement-home tests show a protected DATA_BLOCKED packet with the current connection-level Maximum Data limit after the supported connection send limit is exhausted, no advisory packet is emitted while ack-eliciting work is already outstanding, the advisory packet remains registered for loss repair, existing DATA_BLOCKED frame-codec fuzz coverage remains linked, and the new BenchmarkDotNet Dry case executes.
procedure: Run the focused RFC 9000 S4P1/S13P3/S19P12 requirement-home tests.
Run the `QuicConnectionStreamStateBenchmarks` BenchmarkDotNet suite with the Dry job.
Render and check the touched SpecTrace artifacts.
Confirm that the existing DATA_BLOCKED frame-codec fuzz coverage remains linked and no parser behavior was weakened.
preconditions: The active runtime has 1-RTT packet protection material and an active path.
The stream write can be forced to exceed the current connection-level send limit without exceeding the stream-level send limit.
The existing frame codec can parse and format DATA_BLOCKED frames.

supplemental_sections.heading: Fuzz And Benchmark Scope
supplemental_sections.content: The existing `QuicFrameCodecPart4FuzzSupport.FuzzDataBlockedFrame` coverage remains the fuzz proof for untrusted DATA_BLOCKED parse/format boundaries. The permanent `QuicConnectionStreamStateBenchmarks` suite now includes the connection-level `DATA_BLOCKED` helper branch; Dry validation proves the benchmark harness executes but is not treated as a stable performance baseline.

status_summary: Passed locally for the focused requirement-home coverage, DATA_BLOCKED frame-codec fuzz linkage, BenchmarkDotNet Dry validation, and SpecTrace render/check. The proof remains limited to advisory runtime emission on the supported active path.
